subject: feat(theme): migrate to DaisyUI with dual-layer theming and meta theme-color
body:
Partial migration to DaisyUI per docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md.
DaisyUI semantic tokens now coexist with the existing custom CSS variable
system — incremental replacement to stay low-risk rather than a big-bang
rewrite of the 2000+ line styles.css.

Infrastructure:

1. Installed daisyui@5 (5.5.19) as devDependency. Registered two themes
   via @plugin "daisyui" { themes: lofi --default, black --prefersdark }
   in styles.css. --default / --prefersdark set the initial theme before
   JavaScript runs. Added @layer base { html { color-scheme: light } html.dark
   { color-scheme: dark } } so native form inputs and scrollbars match.

2. Dual-layer theming wired up end-to-end: <html> gains data-theme="black"
   alongside class="dark" default. Flash prevention inline script in
   index.html now sets BOTH .dark class AND data-theme attribute before
   first paint, and also overwrites <meta name="theme-color"> content so
   the PWA status bar matches the active theme from the first frame.

3. Added two <meta name="theme-color"> tags with (prefers-color-scheme: ...)
   media queries. Defaults #ffffff (lofi base-100) and #000000 (black
   base-100). AppContext.darkMode effect now calls setAttribute('data-theme',
   ...) and overwrites both meta tags on every toggle via new module
   constants LIGHT_THEME/DARK_THEME/LIGHT_META_COLOR/DARK_META_COLOR that
   must stay in sync with the inline script.

4. Cross-tab sync already existed (AppContext storage event listener) —
   verified it still applies .dark, data-theme, and meta theme-color
   correctly via the shared darkMode effect.

Component migration (~30 of 39 dark: pairs collapsed):

5. HealthBars, HealthAnomalies, Contributors, Tags, Timing, Progress,
   Discover, Timeline, Health: neutral gray progress-bar rails and hover
   states → bg-base-300 / hover:bg-base-200 / hover:bg-base-300. Health
   security indicators → bg-error/10, border-error/40, text-error
   (DaisyUI error semantic token is the right fit for security status).

6. NOT migrated (data viz category colors per reference doc): Summary.jsx
   stat cards (amber/indigo/pink/purple for distinct metrics) and
   Timeline.jsx complexity badges (purple=high, blue=medium). These are
   data visualization colors with fixed meaning, not theme tokens.

Remaining work tracked in docs/TODO.md — full removal of 202 var(--color-*)
references from styles.css and component class migration to DaisyUI btn /
card / input / badge classes are still pending and can happen incrementally.

Build passes (./node_modules/.bin/vite build). CSS bundle 147.16 KB →
146.06 KB from collapsed dark: pairs.

Tags: theme, daisyui, ui, refactor, pwa
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: minor
